title: Two-stream (quick)
description: Two cold electron beams stream past each other with a tiny k = 1 density perturbation. The counter-streaming setup is the classic testbed for the two-stream instability: electrostatic energy grows exponentially, then saturates as particles trap in the wave.
lookFor: Phase space: two separated beams that broaden and filament over time
Electrostatic energy rises sharply after an initial quiet period
Kinetic energy falls as particles exchange energy with the field
Fast web run (~300 particles, coarse 32-cell grid)

title: Two-stream (strong drive)
description: Same two-beam geometry as the quick case, but with a larger initial perturbation (amplitude 0.008). The instability kicks in sooner and field growth is easier to see in the first half of the run.
lookFor: Earlier and steeper rise in electrostatic energy than the quick case
Stronger field amplitudes in the E(x) animation
More rapid beam mixing in phase space
Good choice if you want visible dynamics without waiting long

title: Two-stream (warm beams)
description: Counter-propagating beams with finite temperature (v_th = 0.12). Thermal spread fills out phase space around each beam while the two-stream mode still grows. Compare with the cold cases to see how temperature changes the instability pathway.
lookFor: Phase-space clouds around ±drift velocity instead of thin lines
Instability still develops, but with more diffuse trapping structures
Finer 64-cell grid resolves slightly more spatial structure
Total energy should stay approximately conserved

title: Landau damping (quick)
description: A warm (v_th = 0.4) electron plasma on a periodic domain is given a small standing-wave density perturbation (sin shift, mode k = 1). Resonant particles near the wave phase velocity absorb field energy, so the |E_k| envelope decays even though the spatial cos(kx) pattern is preserved.
lookFor: E(x) antinode amplitude shrinks while nodes stay fixed
|E_k| plot shows log-scale decay over the run
ESE late/early ratio well below 1 in the analysis summary
Compare with Cold plasma (quick) to isolate physical damping

title: Cold plasma wave (quick)
description: Identical setup to Landau quick except v_th = 0. Without a velocity tail at the resonant speed, there is no Landau mechanism — the launched standing wave should oscillate without sustained envelope decay. Use this as the control when interpreting warm Landau results.
lookFor: |E_k| amplitude stays near its initial level
ESE late/early ratio close to 1 (typically 0.9–1.2)
E(x) pattern oscillates but does not systematically decay
Pair with Landau quick to see warm-vs-cold contrast

title: Landau damping (mode 2)
description: Warm standing wave with spatial mode m = 2 (k = 2 on a 2π domain). Higher-k waves have shorter wavelength and often damp faster in PIC, so |E_k| decay should be more pronounced than the k = 1 Landau quick case.
lookFor: E(x) shows two antinodes per domain length
Fourier panel tracks mode m = 2 amplitude
Typically faster |E_k| decay than k = 1 warm case
Standing-wave nodes remain at fixed positions

title: Electron plasma oscillation
description: Single cold electron species with a small standing-wave perturbation. In the absence of beams or thermal Landau damping, the system supports an undamped electron plasma oscillation at ω ≈ ω_p. A short, lightweight run ideal for watching basic wave physics.
lookFor: Regular oscillation of E(x) and electrostatic energy
Phase space stays near v = 0 with modest spatial bunching
|E_k| envelope roughly constant after initial transients
Short run (300 steps) — good first demo to try

title: Drifting warm beam
description: One warm electron beam (drift v = 0.8, v_th = 0.25) with a standing-wave perturbation. The drifting Maxwellian interacts with the self-consistent field, producing shear and mixing in phase space distinct from the two-stream or pure Landau cases.
lookFor: Phase-space cloud centered near the drift velocity
E(x) and |E_k| evolve with both oscillation and beam spreading
Energy budget: kinetic energy dominates; field energy is a small fraction
Useful for exploring wave–particle motion beyond textbook benchmarks

title: Landau damping (full)
description: Validation-resolution warm Landau case: 8000 particles and a 256-cell grid, matching inputFiles/validation/landauDamping.json. Slower in the browser but produces clean |E_k| decay and the same physics cited in docs/validation.md.
lookFor: Clear |E_k| end/start ratio ≈ 0.2–0.3 (strong damping)
Smooth E(x) snapshots with well-resolved nodes and antinodes
ESE late/early ratio typically ≈ 0.8
Expect longer run time — watch the progress bar

title: Two-stream (full)
description: Full validation two-stream parameters (500 particles per beam, 500 steps, 32-cell grid), matching inputFiles/validation/twoStreamInstability.json. Runs longer than the quick variant and reaches a more developed nonlinear stage.
lookFor: Extended growth and saturation of electrostatic energy
Strong phase-space filamentation and vortex-like structures
Peak ESE many times the initial perturbation level
Good for portfolio screenshots; moderate run time
